questions.question: Define a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA). Provide its formal definition as a 5-tuple.
keyPoints: A DFA is a 5‑tuple (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F)
Q is a finite non‑empty set of states
Σ is a finite input alphabet
δ: Q×Σ → Q is the total transition function, q0 ∈ Q is the start, F ⊆ Q are accepting states

questions.question: Construct a DFA that accepts the language L = {w ∈ {a,b}* : the number of a's in w is even}.
keyPoints: Use two states: q_even (start, accepting) and q_odd (non‑accepting)
On reading symbol 'a' the automaton switches to the other state
On reading symbol 'b' it stays in the current state
The accepting state q_even corresponds to an even count of a's

questions.question: Construct a DFA for the language L = {w ∈ {0,1}* : w ends with the substring '01'}. Provide its state diagram and transition table.
keyPoints: Define three states: q0 (start), q1 (last symbol was 0), q2 (last two symbols were 01, accepting)
Transitions: from q0 on 0 go to q1, on 1 stay in q0; from q1 on 0 stay in q1, on 1 go to q2; from q2 on 0 go to q1, on 1 stay in q0
State diagram: three circles with arrows labelled 0/1 accordingly
Transition table lists δ(q, σ) for each q ∈ {q0,q1,q2} and σ ∈ {0,1}

questions.question: Explain the product construction used to obtain a DFA for the intersection of two regular languages. Apply it to construct a DFA for the language L = {w ∈ {a,b}* : the number of a's is even and w ends with b}.
keyPoints: Given DFAs M1=(Q1,Σ,δ1,q01,F1) and M2=(Q2,Σ,δ2,q02,F2), the product automaton has state set Q1×Q2
Start state is (q01,q02); accepting states are (p,q) where p∈F1 and q∈F2
For the specific languages: use the even‑parity DFA (2 states) and the 'ends with b' DFA (2 states) → product has 4 states, mark the one that is both even and ends with b as accepting
